---
title: "Week 2 Flowchart Exercises"
subtitle: "Visualizing Decision Logic"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true
  pdf:
    toc: true
---

# Flowchart Practice

## Exercise 1: ATM Withdrawal

Create a flowchart for an ATM withdrawal:
1. Get withdrawal amount
2. Check if amount is multiple of $20
3. Check if amount <= account balance
4. Check if amount <= daily limit ($500)
5. If all checks pass: Dispense cash
6. Otherwise: Show appropriate error

Your flowchart:
```
[Space for drawing]














```

## Exercise 2: Grade Calculator Flowchart

Complete this partial flowchart for a grade calculator:

```
     Start
       ↓
   [Get Score]
       ↓
   <Valid Score?>
    ↙ No      ↘ Yes
[Error Msg]    <Score >= 90?>
    ↓           ↙ Yes    ↘ No
    ↓       [Grade = A]   <??>
    ↓           ↓         ↙  ↘
    ↓           ↓    [Continue...]
    
[Complete the flowchart]
```

## Exercise 3: Code from Flowchart

Convert this flowchart to Python code:

```
     Start
       ↓
   [Get Age]
       ↓
   <Age < 4?>
    ↙ Yes  ↘ No
 [Free]    <Age < 12?>
    ↓       ↙ Yes  ↘ No
    ↓   [$5.00]   <Age >= 65?>
    ↓       ↓      ↙ Yes  ↘ No
    ↓       ↓   [$8.00]  [$10.00]
    ↓       ↓      ↓        ↓
    └───────┴──────┴────────┘
              ↓
        [Show Price]
              ↓
            End
```

Your code:
```python
# Convert flowchart to code:





```

## Exercise 4: Parking Meter Logic

Draw a flowchart for parking meter payment:
- If Sunday: Free parking
- If before 8am or after 6pm: Free parking  
- Otherwise: $2 per hour
- Maximum 3 hours
- Must pay before parking

Include:
- Day input
- Time input
- Hours needed
- Payment calculation

Your flowchart:
```
[Space for drawing]














```

## Exercise 5: Debug the Flowchart

This flowchart has logic errors. Find and fix them:

```
     Start
       ↓
   [Get Speed]
       ↓
   <Speed > 65?>
    ↙ Yes   ↘ No
[Ticket]   <Speed > 55?>
    ↓       ↙ Yes  ↘ No
    ↓    [Warning] [OK]
    ↓       ↓       ↓
    └───────┴───────┘
            ↓
          End
```

Problems found:
1. ________________________________
2. ________________________________

Corrected flowchart:
```
[Draw corrected version]







```

## Submission

Take photos of your flowcharts or use a digital tool.
Include:
- All 5 exercises
- Clear, readable diagrams
- Proper flowchart symbols
- Complete logic paths
